item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Brachypelma Elementary School

"Brachypelma is a genus of spiders in the family Theraphosidae (tarantulas), found in Mexico and Central America." Clever…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My thoughts as well. There doesn't seem to be anything that is kicking me harder than usual.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

So, while reading this for Wikiwalk, I was immediately reminded of Tufto's SCP-3979 in the sense that both involving really messed up talking spiders of unknown origin. If was Johnny Foundation, I wouldn't rule out that both are potentially utilizing the same effect, just to different ends. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-2507 designates eleven organisms of the class Arachnida, with an average height of 3 meters and a mass of 54 kilograms. All instances of SCP-2507 are capable of vocalizing in American English, and speak in voices resembling young female children.

To this:

SCP-2507 designates eleven organisms of the class Arachnida, with an average height of 3 meters and a mass of 54 kilograms. All instances of SCP-2507 are capable of vocalizing in American English, and speak in voices resembling young female children.1

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. The similarity of this effect to SCP-3979-2 has been noted, and the potential of a shared origin is currently under investigation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If not provided with entertainment, SCP-2507 will become restless, and begin attempting to exit containment. Phrases such as 'I'm bored', 'when will mom pick us up', 'can we watch cartoons?' and 'you look tasty.' are common.

TBH can relate, same.

also this is really good, i like teenage spiders very much, enjoy the 1+ of a lifetime.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Where did this bit go? I really liked it when I read this pre-membership a year or so back, but it's gone now.

Nevermind, temporarily struck blind by the awesomeness…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not badly done, but not particularly engaging either. Curious that this has such a rating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the kind of thing that could've easily become stupid, but instead works really well. And even manages to be creepy as fuck while also being pretty funny.

Nicely done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The spelling of "Kemberly" makes me want to kick a puppy, but I'm certain there are people who have actually afflicted their daughters with that spelling, so fair enough. Really, child naming in general has made me want to angrily wade through a lake of puppies until I can't kick anymore. At least she isn't named anything ending in "ayden" or homophones thereof. And if you change it to something ending in "ayden", so help me…

Also, anoma-spider puberty is *weird*.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, preteen spiders. Just, wow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In other words, seeking more weirdness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
not doing it for me… too many other similar SCPs. Communist spiders and the court of the fox king, i'm looking at you… and liking you better.

Unless… someone had something linking these into a common mythology…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure if I like this or not…it's not poorly written, though I don't particularly find anything…interesting about it. I don't find it bad and understand the premise, but I don't mind spiders, and don't find pre-teen girls creepy at all. There is no catch for me.
Also, I curse having to google size ratios every time I read an SCP…damn metric system…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like the G.I.F.T.S. if they were all preteen girls. Horrifying. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll second this. It's particularly awkward here

The other instances of SCP-2507 refused to move near it, and for six days they forced SCP-2507 into one corner of the containment chamber.

… where SCP-2507 is used once in the sentence to mean the group, and once to mean a specific individual.

Otherwise, good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weird as hell. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like that they actually are spiders, not just girls in spider bodies.

…How is that sentence even possible in this world.

One thought: giving us Kemberly's name is fine, but I'd recommend tossing in a -1 or something to refer to her by in the rest of the article. Would tighten up tone just a smidge.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was just going to make this exact comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually figured that out post-post but could't be bothered to edit. Thanks though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, that's breast size.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
sharply criticizing the… cephalothorax of the instances lower on the social hierarchy.

That's where I upvoted. The image of adolescent schoolgirl spiders criticizing each other's butts… +1. You deserve it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a pretty good example of how making something just a little bit silly can make it a whole lot more disturbing. I like it!

Alternate responses...
I'm not sorry.
  • I'm really wrapped up in this article.
  • This article really cliques with me on a personal level.
  • That's one fantastic story you've spun there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On 4/25/1987, the SCP-2507 instance was ripped apart and killed by the other instances of SCP-2507, who placed the remains within the shed husk.

This was chilling in chat and it still it. Gone through puberty and then butchered and forced to fill its previous role. My cute-creepy scale is currently seizing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
'I'm bored', 'when will mom pick us up', 'can we watch cartoons?' and 'you look tasty.'

Upvoted as soon as I saw this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What in the….
